Malaya Industries Holdings, Inc. — Internal Cyber Incident Report (Confidential; MNPI) — 03 June 2026, 07:15 PHT
On 02 June 2026 at 21:40 PHT, our SOC detected anomalous admin logins to the cloud file repository hosted by vendor LuzonNet Data Services, Ltd., traced to an external IP range.
Affected systems: HR payroll staging server and Accounts Payable invoice archive; production customer portals and manufacturing control systems remain unaffected and operational.
Personal data potentially exposed include employee names, company email addresses, payroll bank account aliases, and internal staff IDs (format MIH-EMP-####); no government IDs, biometrics, or customer account numbers were stored in the affected folders.
Preliminary, unaudited assessment indicates remediation and downtime costs of approximately PHP 18–22 million and an expected deferral of certain 2Q26 expense postings by up to one week, with a provisional revenue impact of 0.5–1.0%.
Under the PSE Listing and Disclosure Rules and the Securities Regulation Code, this is material; we will file a Current Report on SEC Form 17-C via PSE EDGE before market open on 04 June 2026, with an updated disclosure within the same trading day if scope changes.
To avoid selective disclosure, all external communications must flow through the PSE EDGE posting and SEC filing; press inquiries are to be routed to corpcomms and Legal, and no investor guidance will be issued until the 17-C is posted.
Employees covered by the Insider Trading Policy are reminded that the blackout window remains in effect from 01 June until two full trading days after public release of 2Q26 results; Board review of preliminary impacts is scheduled on 28 June 2026.
Data Privacy Act compliance: the National Privacy Commission will be notified within 72 hours, and affected employees will receive notices by 05 June 2026; dedicated mailbox: dpo@malayaindustries.ph.
Third-party status update: LuzonNet posted a broad regional service advisory on 03 June citing “transit instability”; this is not our disclosure and does not detail issuer-specific impact or remedial actions.
Public references for context: our SEC Form 17-A filed on 15 April 2026 discussed cloud migration timelines, and a brief PSE EDGE notice on 03 June (“temporary service interruption”) has been released; the quantitative impact and system scope in this report remain non-public pending the 17-C.
No effect is expected on tax filings, bank covenant tests, or PDEx program settlements; Isla Pacific Bank, Inc. confirmed payroll clearing is unaffected (case ref IPB-2026-4412).
Points of contact: Ms. Armi de la Cruz, Data Protection Officer, dpo@malayaindustries.ph; Atty. Rico Paglinawan, Corporate Secretary, secdisclosures@malayaindustries.ph; hotline +63 920 555 0198.
